channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: HGV’s ignoring road signs, 18th June
item.description:
An HGV has blocked Pembroke Road for nearly an hour. Cars have been speeding down the footpaths to get around it. Asking them why they came down here, they said their Sat Nav directed them down here and that they didn’t see the road signs that they ended up moving out of the road so that they could manoeuvre out. Due to so many foreign drivers we need a sign with a picture, not a silly little one on a post 8ft into the road or a floor standing written sign that many drivers cannot read

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pembroke Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: I reported the below on the 27th November 2025 report/8474451, 18th June
item.description:
I reported the below on the 27th November 2025 and pass this location most weeks and don't believe it has be resolved .....Beckenham War Memorial roundabout ie junction of Beckenham Road, Rectory Road, High Street and Croydon Road. At Beckenham Road junction Zebra Crossing Belisha Beacon not working tonight. https://fix.bromley.gov.uk/report/8474451
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 251, High Street, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wait button won't depress on this unit., 18th June
item.description:
The adjacent one does work. The wait lights do work - it's just that the one button is jammed. Thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34-48, London Road, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress left outside on walkway, 18th June
item.description:
Can not walk on pathway due to mattress being left will need to be collected as soon as possible due to it being an obstetrician

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A toilet and kids toys dumped on street, 18th June
item.description:
This toilet has been dumped on the street for the last 2 weeks at least.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Eastcombe Avenue, Charlton

item.title: Blocking emergency fire access, 18th June
item.description:
And driveway to 4 garages.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sharp metal protruding on railings, 18th June
item.description:
There is protuding sharp metal between the joint of the two section of railing at the top. This section is on the left hand side after the zebra crossing as you walk toward school and park. Very small but razor sharp. This could slice though skin and damage anyone running there hand along and this is outside a school so quite dangerous.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Churchfields Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Raised pavement slab., 18th June
item.description:
The pavement here is in a bad condition with some raised pavement slabs. My wife tripped up on this, this evening.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fair Acres, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree overhanging balcony, 18th June
item.description:
The tree is blocking almost all of my view and is preventing natural light from reaching my home. It has also obstructed my sight of the surrounding area, making my balcony feel enclosed and unusable. I am an older lady and have COPD, which makes it difficult for me to get out and about. My balcony is one of the few places where I can sit outside and enjoy some fresh air. Unfortunately, because the branches are so close and the tree produces a significant amount of pollen, it affects my breathing and aggravates my COPD symptoms. As a result, I am unable to use my balcony at all. This situation is having a negative impact on both my health and my quality of life. I would be grateful if the tree could be inspected and cut back as soon as possible so that I can safely use my balcony again and regain the light and outlook that I have lost.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cray Avenue, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin, 18th June
item.description:
Bin hasn’t been emptied for st least 4 days

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): London Fields East Side, Hackney, London

item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Abandoned white van, 18th June
item.description:
Van has been in the Council-owned car park of Watson Close (N16 8QH) for approx 5 months, has not been moved since it appeared. No number plates and flat tyres. Residents have asked other neighbours if the van belongs to someone, but no one knows

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Watson Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped suitcase, 18th June
item.description:
See photo

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car - No tax / SORN, 18th June
item.description:
Black Tesla X registration X15 SGC looks dumped at the end of Pinewood Ave at junction with Brookend. What looks like a fake wheel clamp has been applied and a blank fake warning notice on the windscreen. This car has no MOT, expired several months ago, and is registered SORN - so should not be on the road at all.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 107, Pinewood Avenue, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road sign left after roadworks completed, 18th June
item.description:
Road sign has been left after roadworks were completed and all other signage taken away

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greatwood, Elmstead, Greater London

item.title: Dead dog on the “beach”, 18th June
item.description:
Deceased dog spotted on the beach directly in front of the oxo tower. The tide was out so it may have been in the water.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 8th floor, The Queen's Walk, Southwark, Greater London

item.title: It's a dump, 18th June
item.description:
Street cleaning desperately needed. And we need it to be effective, with the cleaning team actually removing the rubbish, not leaving half of it there.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Park Avenue, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet
